Program

Today, approximately 20 thousand different types of medical devices and systems are used in hospitals. The number of biomedical devices and systems used in our country has increased 7 times in the last 20 years.

The need for staff with technical and scientific knowledge to carry out the design and development of biomedical systems and to take part in the efficient use of systems is increasing day by day. In addition to theoretical trainings, the fact that students receive practical training in an equipped laboratory provides an important preparation opportunity before field applications. The devices such as digital analog experiment set, multimeter and oscilloscope available

in the Biomedical Technology Laboratory

established for this purpose primarily provide the infrastructure to be used in the application stages of the basic department courses such as Circuit Analysis, Electronic Measurement Technique, Electronic Elements and Analysis, Fault Analysis and Troubleshooting, which are included in the curriculum to gain general electrical-electronic knowledge.

Biomedical Technology Laboratory Equipment

KL-720 K&H Biomedical Measuring Instrument

ETS-7000A K&H Digital analog experiment system

The Biomedical

Measurement Set

in our Biomedical Technology Laboratory

performs various physiological signal measurements with 9 different physiological measurement modules. Medical devices, the physiological signals they measure, the measurement method and the sensors in its content are explained in a way that includes signal processing steps. Physiological signals from sensors are detected, amplified, filtered and ultimately the measured values are visualized on an oscilloscope or computer environment. It is used to develop students' knowledge, skills and abilities about Biomedical Systems.

Qualifications of Biomedical Device Technicians

Graduates of the biomedical device technology program are given the title of "Biomedical Device Technician".

Biomedical device technician, instruments and devices used in the field of medicine and biological sciences, in accordance with their schemes, install them in their places and maintain them continuously and repair them by determining the location when there is a malfunction.

Those who want to be a biomedical device technician are expected to be successful in the field of science, to be interested in tools and machines, to be able to use their hands and fingers skillfully, to have the ability to perceive mechanical and shape relationships, to have strong visual and hearing organs, to enjoy helping people, to have a strong sense of responsibility.
